title: Refactor Website Settings endpoint to use standard session validation

requirements.text: In the Website Settings backend endpoint (backend/main.mo), remove all existing manual session validation logic including header checks and token parsing

quotes: Remove all existing session validation logic inside this endpoint
Do not manually check headers here
Do not parse token inside this endpoint

acceptanceCriteria: No manual Authorization header parsing exists in the Website Settings endpoint
No manual token validation logic exists in the Website Settings endpoint
No direct session map lookups exist in the Website Settings endpoint

requirements.text: Call validateSession(request) at the start of the Website Settings endpoint exactly as implemented in the Media endpoint

quotes: Call validateSession(request) exactly like Media endpoint

acceptanceCriteria: validateSession(request) is called at the beginning of the Website Settings endpoint
The call pattern matches the Media endpoint's implementation

requirements.text: Use the user Principal returned from validateSession for authorization checks in the Website Settings endpoint

quotes: Use returned user from validateSession for authorization

acceptanceCriteria: The user Principal from validateSession is used for all authorization logic
No alternative authorization mechanisms are used

requirements.text: Return HTTP 401 Unauthorized from the Website Settings endpoint only if validateSession fails
acceptanceCriteria: A 401 status code is returned when validateSession returns an error
No other conditions trigger a 401 response
Successful validateSession allows the endpoint to proceed

constraints: Do not modify the Media endpoint implementation
Preserve the existing validateSession function logic
Maintain the current Website Settings endpoint functionality beyond authentication
nonGoals: Modifying authentication logic in other endpoints
Adding new features to Website Settings
